name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical Process Created
notes:
This Building Block detects new critical processes created. Critical processes refer to ones that can potentially be misused by adversaries to perform malicious activities. The common processes include: powershell, cmd, mshta.

Note: The rule may be tuned by the "Process CommandLine" field. 
Some common "Process CommandLine" keywords that appear in malware are:
findstr
tmp
temp
vbs
regsvr32
command
outfile
dll
http
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical Process Created on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Name") MATCHES '.*?powershell.*' OR LOWER("Parent Process Name") MATCHES '.*?cmd.*' OR LOWER("Parent Process Name") MATCHES '.*?mshta.*' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical Process Created on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success

AND when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Name") MATCHES '.*?powershell.*' OR LOWER("Parent Process Name") MATCHES '.*?cmd.*' OR LOWER("Parent Process Name") MATCHES '.*?mshta.*' AQL filter query
